content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 19, 1992


Ernest A. Scott
11218 Crippen Avenue, #4
Adelanto, CA  92301

Re:  Your Request for Informal Assistance
     Our File No. G-92-152

Dear Mr. Scott:

     This is in response to your letter requesting assistance with
respect to the duties of Adelanto Mayor Ed Dondelinger concerning
his responsibilities under the provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act").1

     The Act requires the Commission to provide formal written
advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or
to that person's authorized representative.  (Section 83114(b).)
The Commission does not provide advice to third parties concerning
another person's duties under the Act.  (Regulation 18329(b)(8).)
It does not appear that you have the authorization of the mayor to
request advice from the Commission at this time.  Consequently, we
are unable to provide advice on the issues you raised in your
letter.

     However, we have enclosed a copy of the Political Reform Act
for your information, and a complaint form should you desire to
file a complaint with the Enforcement Division of the Commission.

     If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        By: John W. Wallace
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JWW:aa
Enclosures

________________
1    Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: It does not appear that you have the authorization of the mayor to request advice from the Commission at this time. Consequently, we are unable to provide advice on the issues you raised in your letter.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the duties of Adelanto Mayor Ed Dondelinger under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the requestor (a third party) lacked authorization from the mayor to seek advice on his behalf. The Act only permits the Commission to provide formal written advice to the person whose duties are in question or their authorized representative.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with no formal Q/A sections. The key legal principle cited is Government Code Section 83114(b) and Regulation 18329(b)(8), which restrict FPPC advice to the subject person or their authorized representative.

QUESTION: What are the duties of Adelanto Mayor Ed Dondelinger under the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: It does not appear that you have the authorization of the mayor to request advice from the Commission at this time. Consequently, we are unable to provide advice on the issues you raised in your letter.

(Section 83114(b).) The Commission does not provide advice to third parties concerning another person's duties under the Act. (Regulation 18329(b)(8).) embedding.summary: The FPPC declined an informal advice request from Ernest Scott regarding Mayor Ed Dondelinger's duties under the Political Reform Act, because Scott was not authorized to request advice on the mayor's behalf. The Commission enclosed a copy of the Act and a complaint form for reference.
